sections.paragraph:
εἰρωνεία, ας, ἡ (Pla. et al.; 2 Macc 13:3) ‘affected ignorance’ used to discomfit an opponent in debate by saying less than one thinks (Pla., Rep. 337a et al.) then pretense, posturing (cp. Aristot. EN 2, 7, 12) τῆς νηστείας affected/postured fasting Dg 4:1.—WBuechner, Her 76, ’41, 339–58; Reader, Polemo 256. DELG s.v. εἴρων.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Her Her = Hermes, Zeitschrift für klassische Philologie—List 6

Reader, Polemo Reader, Polemo , s. Polemo—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 288
